documentation_accuracy_summary: In-code Doxygen on FGame::save/load, the new private FGame::reportLoadError(), and FMainFrame::onOpen (include/strategic/FGame.h, src/FMainFrame.cpp/include/FMainFrame.h) accurately describes the header-first write order, the ordered magic/version validation, the aggregated per-field/per-sub-load error checks, the IStrategicUI/console-fallback routing, and the onOpen abort/reset/no-menu-enable behavior -- verified against the actual diff line-by-line, not merely present.
include/core/FSaveFormat.h documents kSaveMagic/kSaveFormatVersion/kMaxSerializedStringBytes accurately (unchanged in this pass, correctly referenced).

AGENTS.md's save-format bullet (line ~404) and its new Strategic-tests / GUI-tests-invariant bullets (lines ~241, ~243) accurately summarize the completed P5-1 through P5-5 chain and the two new test fixtures, matching the actual implementation and test diffs verified in this review.

doc/synthesized-roadmap.md's H1 and H3 rows and Part III item #5 are correctly marked RESOLVED with accurate per-subtask attribution (P5-1 through P5-5); cross-checked against the actual source diff and matches the plan's documentation-impact guidance for phase completion.

doc/UsersGuide.md's new File > Open note accurately describes the version-tagged format, the reject-with-error-message behavior on a corrupt/incompatible file, the return to the no-game-loaded state, and the resulting one-time save-compatibility break -- matches the shipped onOpen()/load() behavior exactly.

Minor gap (NOTE finding above, non-blocking): the two Security-surfaced follow-up defects are not yet mirrored into doc/deferred-tasks.md, though Security recommended it; low risk since both are captured in committed security_report.md/security_result.json and this verifier report.
test_sufficiency_summary: Strong, genuinely behavioral coverage for every stated acceptance criterion. FGameSaveFormatTest::testSaveEmitsMagicAndVersionHeaderBeforeGameData decodes the first 8 saved bytes as two explicit little-endian uint32_t values (independent of host endianness) and asserts them against kSaveMagic/kSaveFormatVersion -- a true behavioral/byte-level assertion, not source inspection.

testFullRoundTripRestoresRoundCurrentPlayerRetreatConditionLossCountersMapAndPlayers drives real game-state mutation (endSatharTurn/endUPFTurn/endSatharTurn, a co-located raider fleet with one doomed and one surviving ship to force cleanUpShips()), saves, reloads into a fresh FGame, and asserts round/current-player/player-count/names/fleet contents match, plus a byte-for-byte re-save equality check that indirectly proves the private retreat-condition and loss-counter fields (no public getters) round-tripped correctly.

testLoadWrongMagicReturnsNonzeroAndReportsExactlyOnce / testLoadUnsupportedVersionReturnsNonzeroAndReportsExactlyOnce / testLoadTruncatedStreamReturnsNonzeroAndReportsExactlyOnce / testLoadUnknownFactoryTypeReturnsNonzeroAndReportsExactlyOnce each corrupt a real byte stream (bit-flipped magic, forced-invalid version, truncated-after-header, and a ship-type tag swapped to a 9-byte unrecognized string matching the real writeString() wire encoding) and assert rc != 0 plus exactly one RecordingUI::showMessage() call -- real execution, real assertions, no assertContains()-style source-text checks anywhere in this fixture.
testLoadFailureWithNoUIFallsBackToConsoleAndReturnsNonzero exercises the NULL-IStrategicUI console fallback by redirecting std::cout and asserting captured output is non-empty alongside rc != 0.

StrategicGuiLiveTest::testMainFrameOnOpenFailedLoadResetsGameAndLeavesMenuItemsDisabled drives the real FMainFrame::onOpen() through a native file-open dialog and a real wxGenericMessageDialog error report against a genuinely corrupt file, and asserts all seven game-dependent menu items stay disabled -- direct behavioral proof of the resetGame()/no-menu-enable path; it deliberately avoids showing the frame to sidestep the documented pre-existing NULL-FMap paint crash (WARNING finding above), which is an appropriate, well-commented scope boundary rather than a coverage gap for this criterion.
The pre-existing testMainFrameOnOpenConfirmLoadsFromFullPathAndRestoresPostLoadState continues to cover the unchanged success path.
LE byte-decode assertions plus the byte-for-byte re-save equality check jointly satisfy the fixed-width-little-endian acceptance criterion behaviorally.

Confirmed independently in this run: tests/SSWTests -> OK (289 tests), tests/tactical/TacticalTests -> OK (253 tests), tests/gui/GuiTests under xvfb-run -a -> OK (82 tests) -- exact match to the reported make check totals, all green, no failures observed.

Residual, explicitly-documented gap (tied to the FPlayer/FFleet WARNING finding above): deep-truncation-inside-fleet is intentionally not tested since it would fail under current (out-of-scope) code; the truncation test deliberately cuts right after the 8-byte header to exercise only the reads P5-5 itself owns. This is a reasonable, transparent scope boundary, not a defect in P5-5's own test coverage.
